task_completion.description: Verifies the agent navigated to the OpenCAD login page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the OpenCAD login screen loaded in a web browser at the URL http://localhost.

task_completion.description: Verifies the agent successfully logged in using the provided admin credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the OpenCAD dashboard or main interface with indicators of an active admin session, such as admin menus or the admin@opencad.local profile.

task_completion.description: Verifies the agent navigated to the incident types management section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the OpenCAD settings or administration page specifically for managing, viewing, or editing Incident Types.

task_completion.description: Verifies the agent initiated the creation of a new incident type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows an open modal, dedicated page, or form with input fields intended for adding a new incident type.

task_completion.description: Verifies the new incident type 'Equipment Rollover' was successfully created and saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the exact text 'Equipment Rollover' present in the saved list/table of incident types, or a clear success message confirming its creation in the GUI.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent used the web GUI to interact with OpenCAD rather than bypassing it.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser interface being used to navigate the OpenCAD application.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent did not use terminal commands to insert the record directly into the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing MySQL/MariaDB insert commands or database administration tools (like phpMyAdmin) are used to bypass the OpenCAD application.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the final result.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open and actively modifying the DOM to falsely display 'Equipment Rollover'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target database table for incident types is verified as 'incident_types' based on the official OpenCAD-php schema. Note that the task requires GUI interaction to add the incident type.
